title: The Dean Campustown
name: Sandy Fenili
text: Beware of the Dean!   We moved my daughter in a few years ago and the day we moved in we noticed a hole in the sofa.  I reported it right away and the individual at the desk told me they will note the account and nothing else I needed to do.   Upon move out this year we get a bill for $1k. For the sofa and paint. They also mentioned our daughter was given a check list at arrival and had to note everything at that time.   This was not true. I was there.   We shared the pictures of a clean apartment and there was no response and we were sent to collections.   When I went on the U of I parents Facebook page and mentioned this to make people aware, the number of parents that responded that something similar happened to their child was unbelievable.  So beware!  Take pictures and get a lawyer on hand just in case they ignor your pictures too.  There are a lot of better places for less money that are much better.  They are responsive and trustworthy and believe in customer service.

title: The Dean Campustown
name: Brian Fenili
text: My daughter occupied the apartment for 2 years.  At move in, we noticed a small tear in the sofa and reported it to the front desk. We were told not to worry, and they would note the account.  Upon vacating the apartment, we cleaned the apartment spotless, patched every wall ding, and took pictures. We were shocked to receive a bill for almost $1,000.  The bill was to replace the sofa and paint the apartment.  The walls were nothing but normal wear by the bed and trash can, and the sofa was never noted in her account.  We contacted the Dean via email and were informed we should have done a formal inspection report.  SHAM!!! I would not be surprised if the person before my daughter also paid for that same sofa. We have since been sent to collections and are looking to take this forward to a higher level.

title: The Dean Campustown
name: Phoebe flannery
text:
Do not live here. While living here, I had countless interactions with staff that left me in disbelief of how rude I was treated. I work in hospitality myself so I always tried to be extra nice when approaching them, but there were several interactions where I was ignored, had eyes rolled at me, was yelled at, I can go on and on. On top of that, half of the amenities didn't work while I was there but we were never compensated. You figure the rent includes the price to use these amenities but when they're broken for a full semester, you're better off paying less to live somewhere without them because you wont get to use them anyway.

Upon move out I was charged almost $350 in move out fees and damages, if you saw what constitutes $350 you'd laugh out loud because its literally nothing. I understand charging some fees to clean etc. but in no world was $350 appropriate. On top of that, when I called to discuss/dispute these charges I was told they were slammed with similar instances and theyd get back to me, so I left them my name and number and was told I'd get a call back soon. It never came. Then I got another email reminding me that I still had charges due, so I emailed back and reminded them that I was still waiting on their call to dispute the charges. They responded that I was now outside the dispute window and would have to pay the amount in full or it would be sent to collections. I protested because I was waiting for them to call me back as promised, and never once over the phone was I told that I had to dispute over an email before a certain period of time. If I had been told that, I would've done it instead of waiting so long for a phone call that the dispute window closed. Nobody there will give me any reason as to why this wasn't disclosed to me over the phone. They scam college kids out of money. Don't sign here, I promise it's not worth it.
responseFromOwnerText: Phoebe, thank you for taking a moment to share these insights. We strive to provide clear communication and helpful support at all times, so we regret your comments to the contrary. We are eager to follow up with you about your move-out and clear up any confusion about the charges and our dispute policies. Please contact us directly at info@thedeancampustown.com to discuss this anytime. We wish you well.

They will charge you outrageous amounts for either leaving belongings or if it isn’t spotless when you move out. Yes I understand that there should be some penalties for leaving your belongings or a little dust collected from the month you weren’t there but this is crazy? 300+ dollars were charged to my account and all my 3 other roommates as well for these reasons!!!! In what world would it take to 1200 dollars to clean these things when it was already clean??? They shouldn’t be profiting from this this is outrageous. The service over the course of the year has been horrible too but I’m just glad I don’t have to deal with this anymore. The cooking area is a galley-style kitchenette with a range and microwave plus a full size fridge.  Parking was free and in a small lot right outside the building.

​Just a heads-up on two things:
1. ​It's pretty quiet most of the time, but there's a fire department nearby. So you will hear sirens occasionally during the night.
​2. The horizontal blinds let in a ton of light so if you're a sensitive sleeper, pack an eye mask.
​Overall, comfortable and loved having the full-sized appliances!

title: Yugo Urbana Illinois
name: uki
text:
THIS PLACE IS A HUGE SCAM!!!! To start off my experience, when I moved in my apartment was filthy when I moved in. It was clear they don’t clean in between tenants as the previous tenant left some of their things in the apartment and it was absolutely gross inside where walking around without shoes on left the bottom of my feet BLACK!!! It seems like the bear minimum that things should be clean when you move in and that was the first red flag for this place.

The apartment itself is okay, but the furniture like the mattress and couch are crappy and are clearly cut corners. The shared areas in the building are quite disgusting too, where the elevator, mailroom, and the sky deck are consistently dirty and bad smelling. NOT TO MENTION most of the amenities here are a SCAM!! The hot tub has not worked once this year, the parking lot gates constantly break, and much more like the 'free printing' isn't free, the management constantly forgets about their own events they set up and the office will randomly close early with no notice, and the firepit doesn't work unless you ask the management to turn it on which would only be during office hours. The parking lot size seems to change a lot where in the first month of being here I didn’t have any problem parking but now there’s some difficulty and sometimes I can’t even find a spot to park despite PAYING to park in the fenced off lot. Because I guess it’s fine if there’s cars parked in the lot that aren’t supposed to be in there, but you’ll be towed if you park in the hotel spots.

My biggest complaint BY FAR however is the NOISE. For a new building there seems to be absolutely no insulation, where you can very clearly hear outside of your apartment. You can hear people walking or talking down the hall from your apartment, you can hear dogs barking, the people above you stomping around or moving furniture, and you can hear outside like your window is always open. The apartment doors here for some reason will always SLAM shut unless you carefully close them and you will hear the SLAMMING during ALL HOURS OF THE DAY. I have filed numerous noise complaints about hearing peoples dogs barking at 8 IN THE MORNING before I have to go to my morning classes because I have been woken up countless times from the barking. I’ve had enough! It’s not even just one person but what sounds like three different arrogant people who can’t keep their dogs quiet during the listed quiet hours. The management here is a joke and has pushed me off every single time I've complained about the noise. I sent them PROOF that this is happening and they did NOTHING to stop it, the last time I called and demanded they do something about it the manager essentially told me to get bent. Clearly this place does not care about its residents once they sign and only care about getting the next flock of sheep in.

Please take this review and RUN AND SIGN SOMEWHERE ELSE!!!

title: Yugo Urbana Illinois
name: Aaron E.J O'brien
text:
I lived in this place for a year, and I really enjoyed my time here. The apartment is fully furnished, and everything is very convenient. Here are some of the good things about this apartment:

(1) Safety: The safety is excellent. You never have to worry about losing your parcels, as there is an Amazon Hub inside the building. The staff at Yugo will also take care of your packages.

(2) Study Room: The study room is very nice and accessible 24 hours a day. Additionally, the Bakelab cafeteria is located on the first floor, making it easy to grab a coffee whenever you want.

(3) Modern Building: The building is new, and everything is designed in a modern style.

(4) Gym and Parking: The gym is available 24 hours a day. If you prefer running outside at night, you can also use the parking lot, which is set up with barriers (only tenants can enter, so you can safely exercise outside, even at midnight).

* Now I'm moving to New York State now, and the rent prices are much higher there. If you want amenities like these (fully furnished apartments, gym, study room, etc.), in New York State, it would cost around $3,000. So, the rent here is quite reasonable.

** The only reason I didn't give 5 stars is that my room didn’t have windows, which was unpleasant, especially when cooking. If you choose this apartment, everything will be great, but make sure to check if your room has windows.
responseFromOwnerText: We really appreciate the kind review, Aaron. Thank you for being such an awesome part of our community and please let us know if there's anything we can do to make your experience even better! – The Yugo Urbana Illinois Team

Even I sent several emails.

I am not the only one who lost things! Last time there was a student who had the same issue, he lost more items than me! Will sue them! Hopefully I can get back my money.

This management is completely unprofessional and irresponsible. If you're thinking about living here, beware! They do not take responsibility for their mistakes and have shown a complete lack of respect for their tenants' rights and belongings. Save yourself the headache and look elsewhere!

title: Yugo Urbana Illinois
name: D em
text:
Please don’t live here. Otherwise, you would regret for the whole year. The worse apartment I’ve stayed in Champaign

1. The management team is uneducated and can’t answer any of your questions. The communication sucks. Whenever you have emergency apartment problems and wanna get help. All they will say is I’ll go ask my manager and never answer back. Some people will directly hang up your phone when you reach out for questions. I was once trapped in the elevator for two hours and nobody came even after I called the office...In the end some other residents help me to get out. Terrible management team!!!
2. The sound insolation is terrible. You could easily hear people's normal activities such as talking and walking during the night
3. Lots of unexpected costs. Other than the expensive rent, there is an additional fee for insurance and cable commission each month which many other student apartments don’t charge.  Also the utility costs is oddly high (about at least $70 / month for 1 person) during the winter.   And it remained high during winter break while I was not even in my apartment.

The apartment and furniture are decent. But there are too many add-up problems you must deal on your own in the long term.  It’s better to think twice before you move in.

title: Yugo Urbana Illinois
name: James Hickman
text:
Part 1. Gather Illinois presents a pretty face that hides the gross incompetence of its staff and shoddy workmanship. Having been at UIUC for over 7 years, I can confidently say this is the worst living experience I have had.

To begin, the first red flag was that I found someone pretending to sublease a unit for a discounted price on Facebook. When I asked to view the place, she said she wasn’t sure how to go about that, to contact the office. It turns out that she was the leasing manager. The discounted rate was actually in the form of an “incentive agreement” to provide a $1500.00 gift card that “could be used towards rent.” To date, I haven’t been able to confirm that, because I haven’t received it, despite the contract stating I’d receive it “within 30 days of move-in.”  It has been over 30 days. The company hasn’t responded to my emails. The manager is always busy or out of the office when I call, and his staff tells me they don’t know when he’ll be in when I ask. When I finally found him in his office. He said it is out of his hands. He tried to say I didn’t move in when I moved in, etc. Finally he promised it would be no later than “Thursday” which has now passed.  Again, he was unavailable and hasn’t responded to my email.

As for the building. I didn’t receive most of my furniture until after a month of moving in. I'm still missing things under the bed. Every time I asked, they kept telling me they emailed me about it, but I told them I hadn’t received one. Other units, still haven’t received their furniture. Again, promise after promise, one false reassurance after another.

Regarding the furniture, it is hurriedly put together by minimum wage workers on the concrete outside, often blocking the door. When not distracted by them fighting with each other or complaining to you about the manager, you can see the surface of your tables, desks, etc. being dragged around the cement. Keep in mind that these random workers that complain to you outside about how little they get paid have been randomly entering our apartments without notice and even while we’re away. I will come home one day and something will be installed. I see them walking in and out of other people’s apartments. There’s a reason they require renters’ insurance. Keep track of your belongings. This rushed and unprofessional work is also seen in your apartment.

The floors of my unit have deep scratches in them from things being dragged around during assembly. The cabinets and shelving also have scratches on them. Keep in mind this is a brand-new building. Drywall work is shoddy at best. There are corners that aren’t even squared, areas where they simply painted over the drywall tape prior to proper sanding. Also, If you care about noise, look elsewhere. Perhaps they cut costs on insulation or forgot it completely as another oversight. This is the loudest building I’ve lived in during my 7 years at university. You can hear the people above you and beside you. You’ll wake to doors slamming in the halls (the doors self-slam when they shut automatically) and you can hear even quiet footsteps and conversations happening down the busy halls.

I attempted to transfer units within 24 hours of move-in. They told me I could. That I’d hear back Thursday. I didn’t. I filed a formal request online. No response. I went in, again, and they assured me this could happen, that the other unit wasn’t finished. The next time I followed up, they pretended those conversations never happened and told me it was leased and they had no units to transfer me to. 3 weeks later, I finally received a response to my formal request, denying it.
responseFromOwnerText: We regret that you have had an unpleasant experience. Resident satisfaction is extremely important to us. Please give us the opportunity to rectify your concerns and change your opinion. We’ll be reaching out shortly to set up a time to meet in person.

title: Seven07
name: Wen Ellen
text:
The property management here is extremely irresponsible. I often see homeless people staying overnight inside the apartment complex, and management never takes any action it feels unsafe.

What’s even more ridiculous is that I’ve already paid for my parking spot, but the parking tag they gave me often doesn’t work, so I can’t even get into the garage. When I went to the front desk for help, they told me, “I can’t offer you any advice.” I honestly can’t believe it — I’ve paid for parking, yet I get locked out of the garage, and they think it’s not their problem? No one tried to help or fix the issue. That’s just absurd.

I have been consistently paying for parking every month and have a valid parking pass. However, the apartment never issued me a required parking sticker. Despite this, the towing company they work with only recognizes the sticker — and my car was almost towed because of this.

Dont‘t rent this apartment
responseFromOwnerText:
Dear Wen,
Thank you for reaching out and sharing your experience. We sincerely appreciate the opportunity to address your concerns and ensure your time at Seven07 is comfortable and stress-free.
We want to reassure you that we have on-site personnel conducting regular patrols throughout the building during all after-hours shifts. We take the safety of our community very seriously and continue to monitor these patrols to maintain a secure environment for all residents.
Regarding parking, we want to ensure your access is seamless. We recently upgraded our entire parking system this year to improve reliability. While the system is currently fully operational and we haven't had reports of issues from other residents, it is possible your specific tag simply needs to be synced with the new software. We have also sent out several email reminders regarding the window stickers required by the towing company to ensure our residents' spots are protected.
Please stop by the front desk or email us at leasing@liveseven07.com so we can personally test your tag, get it synced, and provide you with your required sticker. We are more than happy to help get this resolved for you immediately!
Best regards,
The Seven07 Management Team

title: Seven07
name: Mary J
text: Would give 0 stars if I could. This place says they are luxury apartment when in reality they barely do anything for their residents. Often times when I request maintenance, things are not fixed. On top of that I recently found out that the student workers who run the front desk have master keys to every apartment and can just walk in???? I have heard that they will use these master keys to randomly break into residents apartments because they wanted to see the people that live there - which is a complete violation of privacy.  And no, I am not talking about the mandated inspections. I am referring to front desk workers using their master keys in an unprofessional way that is a complete violation of privacy. I do not believe we should be trusting young college students with running an apartment complex. On top of that packages are always lost (as the young college students who work there are always on their phones instead of actually organizing packages). Stay away from Seven07. You will get scammed and you will not feel comfortable or safe living here.
responseFromOwnerText:
Mary, 

Thank you for taking the time to share your concerns. We take resident feedback and safety very seriously, and we want to clarify a few important points:
Regarding unit access: Student staff do not have unrestricted master key access to apartments. During scheduled unit inspections—which have been communicated weekly for over a month—our student team is assigned specific units on approved inspection lists and is authorised to enter only those units for that purpose. No one is permitted to enter resident units outside of these scheduled and communicated inspections.
Regarding inspections: These inspection rounds are part of our standard building maintenance and safety procedures, and all residents have been notified on a weekly basis for over a month.
Regarding packages:
FedEx and UPS: These carriers deliver directly to resident doors. If a package from FedEx or UPS is missing, residents will need to contact that provider directly using the tracking number.
USPS: USPS deliveries are handled differently. If a package is small enough, the postal carrier may place it directly inside the resident’s mailbox. Larger USPS packages are delivered to the front desk, where we log them and notify residents. If you are unsure, please come see us with your USPS tracking number and we will verify our records.
Regarding phone usage by staff: Our student team also manages our social media presence, which at times requires phone usage to create and post content. This practice is part of their job responsibilities and does not detract from resident service or office professionalism.
Regarding maintenance: If you have any maintenance requests that you feel were not resolved satisfactorily, we encourage you to reach out directly so we can review and follow up promptly.
We care deeply about resident safety, comfort, and trust, and we welcome the opportunity to speak with you privately to ensure your concerns are addressed thoroughly and accurately.
Thank you,
Seven07 Management
